observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal GSO to:
a. Prepare and maintain the prescribed forms, registry, and reports such as SPC,
RegSPI, RSPI, RLSDDSP and IIRUSP, for the establishment of proper control
and accountability over the semi-expendable properties; and
b. Report in the RPCSP all semi-expendable properties including those owned by
the Municipality from the previous years.

In addition, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to maintain the Semi-Expendable Property Ledger Card (SPLC) to record the acquisition,
description, custody, impairment, issue/transfer/disposal, repair history, and other
information about the property.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and
Municipal Accountant to ensure the timely remittance of the barangays’ RPT share
within five days after the end of each quarter, in strict compliance with Section
271(d) of the LGC and Section 15 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal GSO to conduct
a physical inventory of supplies and prepare the required RPCI for timely
submission to the Auditor.

Additionally, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal GSO to conduct periodic reconciliation of property and
accounting records to ensure that inventories are accurately recorded and
reported.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
Direct the MT to strictly comply with the daily remittance and prompt reporting of all collections from AFs, as required by PD No. 1445, RA No. 7160, and the MNGAS for LGUs;
Issue a formal memorandum reminding all collectors and treasury staff that collections from AFs must be remitted and reported no later than the next working day after receipt, without exceptions; and
Require that the monthly reconciliation of AFs be conducted using the Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) and Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) and ensure that any discrepancies are promptly flagged and reported directly to him for appropriate action.

To sustain this commendable performance, we recommended and the Management agreed to adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002, and other related regulations concerning the granting, utilization, and liquidation of CAs.  Continued compliance will help reinforce financial discipline, uphold audit readiness, and institutionalize good governance practices across all levels of the organization.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to take the following actions:
Direct the MAO to conduct a reconciliation of the Due to LGUs account with the records of the Barangays/PGS.  Thereafter, remit immediately the reconciled balance to the LGU concerned;
For the Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable with adequate documentation amounting to ₱241,081.55, coordinate with the respective suppliers to verify the current status of obligations and prioritize their settlement to effectively clear these outstanding liabilities; and
For the Accounts Payable and other liabilities discussed above outstanding for two years or more with lacking supporting documents/records amounting to ₱250,876.26, initiate efforts to locate or reconstruct the necessary records.   Should documentation remain unavailable after thorough attempts and the conditions for reversion exist, reverse/adjust the same to the unappropriated surplus of the GF.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Carefully evaluate the proposed priority development PPAs under the 20% DF to ensure they are ready for implementation or procurement, minimizing the risk of changes, delays, or non-implementation;
Prioritize the timely execution and early initiation of procurement for all PPAs, ensuring adequate time for bidding, awarding contracts, and resolving any unforeseen issues, thereby facilitating the completion of projects on schedule by year-end; and
Review unimplemented PPAs promptly to assess their feasibility.  If a project cannot be implemented, take immediate action to revise or replace it with a viable alternative, ensuring that new projects can be processed early and completed by year-end.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
Direct the MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with Items 5.1.2 and 5.1.15 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 in order to come up with a complete and comprehensive plan that will serve as basis in the implementation of the MGSM’s DRRM PPAs;
Enjoin the MDRRMO to exert more effort in implementing actions and measures pertaining to all aspects of DRRM towards building a disaster-resilient LGU and communities; and
Direct the concerned offices to prepare and maintain complete and updated records on LDRRMF and to monitor closely the utilization and implementation of all PPAs under the said fund to facilitate the timely reprogramming and reallocation of the fund for its optimum utilization.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MDRRMO and the MA to prepare and submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis to the AT within the prescribed period.
Likewise, we recommended, and the Management agreed that the MA observe diligence in the preparation and maintenance of SLs for the unutilized DRRMF in accordance with Section 5.1.12 of COA Circular No. 2012.002.

We recommended and Management agreed to prioritize the filling up of vacant plantilla positions and resort to hiring of casual or emergency staff in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of CSC MC No. 40, series of 1998 and Section 211(b) of the GAAM, Volume I, in order to avoid hiring significant number of JO personnel.
Likewise, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Human Resources and Management Officer to:
Prioritize the formulation of a “Qualification Standards and Salary Rates for JO Positions” to come up with a HRP to be submitted to the SP for subsequent enactment of a resolution adopting the same;
Issue a uniform policy guideline on the hiring of JO personnel to properly determine the necessity of hiring JOs and to strengthen internal controls pertaining thereto; and
Properly prepare JO contracts for the hiring of necessary JO personnel and indicate therein the specific duties, functions and deliverables expected from them as basis in evaluating their accomplishment reports and the propriety of payments to them, pursuant to existing laws, rules and regulations.
Further, we recommended and Management agreed that the Committee on Human Resource of the Sangguniang Bayan to take appropriate action to address all matters pertaining to the organization and management; personnel administration, position classification and pay plan, staffing patterns; and creation of positions and policy formulation, in connection with the observations noted in audit.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the GFP to prepare and submit a supplemental or revised GPB to the DILG Provincial Office whenever there are realignments or newly implemented GAD-related PPAs not originally included in the approved GPB.  This ensures proper documentation, monitoring, and compliance with the provisions of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMCs.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation and the LCE agreed to set clear development goals for the GAD database, regularly monitor its progress, and gather feedback to ensure it effectively meets the Municipality’s needs.
Moreover, we recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the GFP to attend more GAD-related trainings and instruct the GFPS to allocate budget in the GPB for the training on the utilization of HGDG tools to establish more reliable GPB and GAR and more efficient implementation of GAD.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to:
Reorganize the GFPS with emphasis on clear delineation of roles, responsibilities, and functions of both ExeCom and TWG; and
Create a GAD M&E Team to facilitate the evaluation of the outcome of the Municaplity’s GAD policies, programs and projects and to determine areas for improvement for mainstreaming activities.

We commended the Management for their dedicated efforts and strong commitment to full compliance with withholding and remittance obligations for BIR, GSIS, HDMF, and PhilHealth contributions.  Their prompt and accurate remittances of inter-agency payables demonstrate sound financial discipline and robust internal controls.
To sustain this positive performance, we recommended and the Management agreed to continue adhering strictly to all relevant laws and deadlines for withholding and remittances.  On-going monitoring, regular updating of remittance schedules, proper documentation, and periodic training of responsible personnel will further ensure continued compliance and enhance the MGSM’s overall financial management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. provide Allowance for Impairment for the assessed/evaluated non-collectible receivables, in compliance with the pertinent provisions of IPSAS 29; and b. facilitate request for write-off of the remaining dormant loans receivables by conducting the necessary procedures prescribed in Item 7.0 of COA Circular No. 2023-008.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. OIC-Municipal Engineer/OIC-Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator/Chairperson of the Bids and Awards Committee to: ● effectively and judiciously plan the priority development projects to be included in the Annual Investment Program (AIP) by indicating specific time schedule for each procurement activity throughout the year and considering its feasibility and necessity and which could be strictly implemented for a given timeframe; ● provide status of each project as of March 31, 2024 submitted the Audit Team; ● conduct thorough detailed engineering activities in the planning of development projects and ensure that issues encountered in previous years will be prevented to recur for timely implementation/completion of the project pursuant to the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184; and ● evaluate the necessity of the unimplemented projects, especially those from previous years and under the continuing appropriations and consider if reprogramming to another development projects which will cater the needs of the constituents is warranted; and b. OIC-Municipal Human Resource Management Officer (HRMO) to prioritize the filling-up of the plantilla position of the Municipal Engineer for the effective and efficient implementation of the projects under the 20 percent LDF.

observations.recommendation:
We commended Management’s efforts in ensuring prompt implementation of completed PPAs indicating efficient and timely utilization of fund transfers received from various agencies intended for the welfare of its constituents. 
 
Nevertheless, we recommended that the LCE 

Require the MA to retrieve the information for the funds without detailed records.  If all efforts have been exhausted and no records can be found, the Municipality should request a write-off of the accounts; 

Require the Municipal Engineer (ME) to meticulously plan the implementation phase of PPAs in order to optimally utilize the grants received from the Source Agencies within the agreed timeframe, ensuring that the benefits can be immediately enjoyed by the recipients; and 
 
Request assistance from the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG) to recover the funds that were returned to the Bureau of the Treasury (BTr). Additionally, seek alternative funding to replenish the returned funds.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MA and MT to coordinate and closely monitor the submission of financial reports within the set deadlines as stated in Sections 70 and 73 of the MNGAS for LGUs. 

Likewise, we suggested that the MT to submit the liquidation report, report of collection, and report of disbursement to the MA at least five days before the deadline. This will enable the MA to generate and submit the financial reports on time.

We recommended that the LCE:

Require the Municipal Planning and Development Council to ensure optimum utilization of the 20% DF and implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to deliver timely benefits to the constituents; and  

Instruct the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Engineer to plan properly and efficiently all procurement activities pertaining to these developmental PPAs to fast-track the implementation and completion thereof.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (DRRM) Officer to continuously monitor the implementation of the targeted PPAs funded by Local DRRM Fund (LDRRMF) for the optimal utilization thereof in accordance with Section 21 of RA No. 10121.

We recommended that the LCE direct:

The Municipal DRRM Officer to ensure that the LDRRMF Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) includes all available fund balances from the current year as well as previous years.; and

The MA to ensure that the transfer of unspent balances of the Municipal DRRM Fund-Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses to the Special Trust Fund account is completed on or before December 31 each year.

and necessity and which could be strictly implemented for a given
timeframe;
• provide status of each project as of March 31, 2024 to the Audit Team;
• conduct thorough detailed engineering activities in the planning of
development projects and ensure that issues encountered in previous years
will be prevented to recur for timely implementation/completion of the
project pursuant to the pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184; and
• evaluate the necessity of the unimplemented projects, especially those from
previous years and under the continuing appropriations and consider if
reprogramming to another development projects which will cater the needs
of the constituents is warranted.
b. OIC-Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to prioritize the filling-up
of the plantilla position of the Municipal Engineer for the effective and efficient
implementation of the projects under the 20 percent LDF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Human Resource
Management Officer to immediately adopt the ATM payroll system to safeguard
government funds from risk of loss or misapplication associated with the manual
payroll system.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor institute measures in the current payroll
system by appropriate segregation of duties of personnel assigned for processing,
reviewing, recording, custody, and approval/authorization of transactions.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Human
Resource Management Officer to assume the function of payroll
preparation/computation from the Municipal Budget Officer to eliminate the risks
associated with improper segregation of duties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Credit
    Committee to immediately collect the receivables from the borrowers by
    continuously sending demand letters and strictly implementing other available
    remedies to increase the collection and the funds available that can be used to
    deliver basic services to its constituents pursuant to Section 2 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and
    Municipal Assessor to expedite the processing of the Transfer Certificate of
    Title (TCT) for the 16 lots owned by the Municipal Government of Magdalena in
    compliance with Article 47 (c), Rule IX of the IRR of RA No. 7160 and Section
    148 of the RRSPM in the Local Government prescribed under COA Circular No.
    92-386 to protect the interest of the Municipality from possible third-party
    claims, land disputes and unlawful occupants.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Inventory Committee
    and the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to:
    a. strictly comply and follow guidelines and procedures for the one-time
    cleansing of PPE account balances as prescribed under COA Circular No.
    2020-006;
    b. immediately undertake an appraisal of the unserviceable properties and
    prepare a corresponding report thereon, and facilitate the immediate
    disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with existing rules
    and regulations; and
    c. submit to COA Auditor a request for disposal of all unserviceable
    properties together with the Inventory and Inspection Report of
    Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), Appraisal Report, and other required
    documents for appropriate action.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
    a. former Municipal Treasurer to submit written explanations/ justifications
    for the noted deficiencies;
    b. Municipal Health Officer, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal Accountant to
    reconcile the Municipality’s records against the Philhealth’s records
    and update their records/reports/books, if necessary;
    c. AO under Municipal Health Office to properly prepare and accomplish the
    RCD as supported by the duplicate copies of ORs and Auto Credit

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the annual physical inventory of all PPE items and mobilize additional competent personnel from other departments to assist and facilitate the conduct thereof;
Require the Office of the MA to maintain CIP Ledger Card and complete and updated PPELCs to support GL accounts balance and the MGSO to keep corresponding PCs for control purposes; and
Strengthen the controls on the handling and recording procedures for the PPE accounts by performing periodic reconciliation of records between the MGSO and Accounting Office and ensure that any discrepancies noted are immediately investigated and adjusted in the books accordingly.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Instruct the former Municipal Engineer to prepare a written explanation to justify the additional costs included in the computation of the ABC and submit the same to the AT, for further evaluation; and
Request the contractor to submit to the AT, copies of the official receipts for the actual cost incurred for Structural Analysis, for validation.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the contractor to correct the defects noted or undertake the necessary reconstruction works for the said defects and demand full reimbursement of cost incurred in accordance with the pertinent provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the contractor to finish the construction of the project Repair/Rehabilitation of San Sebastian Barangay Health Station and impose the liquidated damages, if warranted.
We also recommended and Management agreed to monitor properly all projects implemented and undertaken by contractors in order not to delay the delivery of benefits to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the MA to confirm the balances of all the Due to NGAs accounts and/or make representation to the concerned SAs to reconcile their respective records.
Otherwise, Management shall return the said unutilized funds to the Bureau of Treasury, if the documents or records of the specific accounts cannot be located or traced anymore.

We commended Management for its notable work accomplishments relative to the implementation of PPAs under the 20% DF.  Likewise, we recommended and Management agreed to further improve the planning and prioritization of PPAs to be funded by the 20% DF by giving importance also to other areas/aspects of development for holistic improvement of the municipality.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the MDRRM Council to ensure that PPAs funded by the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF are incorporated in the LDRRMFIP pursuant to Section 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We commended Management’s efforts in ensuring the gender-responsiveness, correctness and alignment of the entries contained in its GPB by obtaining the certificate of review and endorsement from the DILG Sorsogon Provincial Office.
On the other hand, we recommended and the GFPS agreed to:
Ensure timely submission of GAD-related reports to the AT in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 2014-001; and
Maintain compliance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01 on the preparation and submission of GAD-related reports.

We recommended and the GFPS agreed to:
Assist the Local Sanggunian in formulating the GAD Code to ensure that GAD is mainstreamed in the local policy-making;
Establish the GAD database by gathering sex-disaggregated data, placing priority on the data relevant to the GAD issues indicated in the GPB to ensure that the said issues represent actual gender gaps and/or differences among the constituents of the Municipality; and
Allot the necessary and adequate budget in formulating the GAD Code and in setting-up and maintaining the GAD database.

We recommended and Management agreed to provide adequate funds for the PPAs that will address the needs of its constituents, particularly the COVID-19 pandemic if the cases will continue to rise.
Also, we recommended and the Management agreed to strengthen the linkage between the planning and budgeting process to optimize the utilization of resources, taking into consideration the key issues in the Municipality.

We commended Management for having extended assistance to the National Government through faithful compliance of its duties as withholding and remitting agent of taxes in accordance with tax laws, rules and regulations during the current year.
We likewise recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to facilitate the reconciliation of prior years’ withheld taxes with the amounts remitted to determine the actual amounts due to BIR.  Thereafter, prepare the necessary adjusting entry to correct the Due to BIR account.

We commended Management for extending assistance to the Government Owned and Controlled Corporations (GOCC) through faithful compliance of duties as withholding and remitting agent of premiums/contributions and loan amortizations in accordance with relevant laws, rules and regulations during the current year.
Nevertheless, we recommended and the LCE agreed to require the MA to conduct detailed review and analysis of the unreconciled balances in the inter-agency payable accounts, including confirmation with the respective GOCCs, to determine the correct balance of the said accounts and thereafter, effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management to:
Include as part of the sources of DRRMF the financial assistance amounting to ₱3.84 million and its corresponding disbursement/charges in the preparation of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in the succeeding period;

Henceforth, ensure that all financial assistance/grants received by the Municipality, whether from other government agencies or local donors, shall be presented as part of sources of DRRMF and its disbursements included in the utilization portion of the report; and

Require the LDRRMO in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit through the LDRRM Council and the Local Development Council, the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and other DRRM resources.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure that only those expenses related to the implementation of DRRM activities should be charged against the LDRRMF;

Consider the augmentation of the regular budget of the MDRRM Office in order to avoid inappropriate charging in the Calamity Fund; and

Strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and other applicable law, rules and regulations governing the utilization of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the concerned offices furnish the MDRRMO officer with the necessary data relative to the unexpended balances from prior years, so that immediate inclusion of it in the LDRRMFIP will be made.
We recommended that Management equip the MDRRMO personnel with the necessary trainings that will help them in the efficient and effective management of DRRM-related projects and activities.
observations.reason: Due to time-constraint, the MDRRMO personnel still have not yet completed all the required DRRM trainings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the BAC to submit promptly all contracts with complete supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five working days from the perfection thereof in compliance with the cited rules and regulations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant
and Municipal Treasurerto:
a. to identify and reconcile the discrepancies noted totaling P102,286.93 and
complete the preparation of inventory records for all PPE accounts to ascertain the
accuracy of the PPE balances reported in theFinancial Statements; and
b. to prepare completely and keep updating the Property Cards per category of PPE
of all the property ownedby theMunicipality;

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to
prepare completely and keep updating the Property Cards per category of PPE of all
the property ownedby theMunicipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant
to:
a. continue issuing demand letters for the settlement of outstanding receivables;
b. set-up the corresponding Allowance for Impairment in the books of accounts based
on the assessed collectability of the long outstanding receivables; and
c. gather the necessary documents required under COA Circular No. 2016-005 to be
able to file a request for write off and if granted derecognize the related
uncollectible accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer,
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to exert all efforts in locating the
Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) between the OP and the Municipality of
Magdalena; otherwise, come up with all the possible remedies to enforce
settlement of the loan for a fair presentation of the account in the Financial
Statements.

We recommended thatthe Municipal Mayor instruct the:
• Municipal Accountant to:
a. maintain a complete and updated Supplies Ledger Cards for each type
of supplies andmaterials; and
b. record the issuances based on the Summary of Supplies and Materials
Issued (SSMI) instead of theRIS; and
• Municipal Treasurer and other concernedpersonnel to:
a. monitor and record the receipts and issuances of all types of
inventories before its distribution to the end-users and maintain Stock
Cards foreach type ofinventories/supplies;
b. prepare and submit monthly the SSMI to the Municipal Accountant for
the recording of issuances; and
c. prepare and submit the RPCI to report the physical count of
supplies/materials bytype ofinventory as of agiven date.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to
record the deposits and disbursements totaling P1,157,128.76 and P885,648.75,
respectively, in the two cashbooks maintained under the Trust Fund and
thereafter reconcile the balances with the Accounting records, in compliance
withSections 13 of the MNGAS forLGUs, Volume I.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare accounting entries to take up the cancellation of stale checks in the Books of
Account of the Municipality by debiting Cash-in-Bank, LCCA and crediting Accounts
Payable in the amount of P3,494.00, in compliance to Section 59, Chapter 3 of the
MNGAS for LGUS, Volume I.

We recommended thatthe Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Appraisal and Disposal Committee to immediately undertake an appraisal of these
unserviceable properties and prepare a corresponding report thereon; and facilitate
the immediate disposal of the unserviceable properties in accordance with existing
rules and regulations to prevent further deterioration due to continuous exposure to
elementsthereof thatwoulddecrease thesalvage value of theproperties;
b. Municipal Treasurer to submit to COA Auditor a request for disposal of all
unserviceable property together with the Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), Appraisal Report and other required documents
forappropriate action; and
c. Municipal Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to drop from the
books of accounts the sold/condemned properties upon disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned officials to
submit immediately the lacking documents for transactions totaling
P9,549,691.92 to the Audit Team within 15 days for further review and
evaluation; and henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 R-
IRRofRA No. 9184, Section 4 of PD No. 1445and COACircular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
ensure the sufficiency of the warranty securities posted by the contractors to cover
any “structural defects and failures” from final acceptance of the project up to the
period prescribed in Section 62.2.3.2 of the R-IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed to continue to pursue improved practices in programming, prioritizing and implementing programs and projects to maximize the utilization of the 20 per cent DF in attaining desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes for the benefit of the community.

We recommended that Management:
Include as part of the sources of DRRMF the financial assistance amounting to ₱3.84 million and its corresponding disbursement/charges in the preparation of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF in the succeeding period;
Henceforth, ensure that all financial assistance/grants received by the Municipality, whether from other government agencies or local donors, shall be presented as part of sources of DRRMF and its disbursements included in the utilization portion of the report; and
Require the LDRRMO in coordination with the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit through the LDRRM Council and the Local Development Council, the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and other DRRM resources.

We recommended that Management coordinate with the Source Agency and seek further clarification as to whether or not the project rehabilitation of the Municipal MRF was in accordance with the MOA. Thereafter, notify the Audit Team of the result of the same so that further verification can be made on the subject transaction.
We also recommended that Management refrain from implementing projects sourced from grants received from other government agency and other local donors which are not in accordance with the MOA or the specific purpose for which the fund was given.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure that only those expenses related to the implementation of DRRM activities should be charged against the LDRRMF;
Consider the augmentation of the regular budget of the MDRRM Office in order to avoid inappropriate charging in the Calamity Fund; and
Strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and other applicable law, rules and regulations governing the utilization of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the concerned offices furnish the MDRRM Officer with the necessary data relative to the unexpended balances from prior years, so that immediate inclusion of it in the LDRRMFIP will be made.
We recommended and Management agreed to equip the MDRRMO personnel with the necessary trainings that will help them in the efficient and effective management of DRRM-related projects and activities.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the BAC to submit promptly all contracts with complete supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five working days from the perfection thereof in compliance with the cited rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed to exert extra effort to collect the excess subsidies from the concerned beneficiaries, and thereafter return the said amounts to the Bureau of the Treasury. On the other hand, the Audit Team appreciates Management’s efforts on its substantial compliance relative to the receipt and timely distribution of the subsidies to the identified eligible families within the Municipality.

We recommended that Management review its accounting and reporting system starting from the determination of an accounting transaction, manner of recording in the journals as well as posting of the transactions to the ledgers up to the preparation of the FS in order to check and verify the possible erroneous recordings or postings made in the books. Thereafter, immediately correct and rectify all errors and inaccuracies observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: Municipal Accountant to: a. adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for the receipts and issuances of supplies, in compliance with Sections 114 and 121 of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; b. record the issuances thereof based on the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) submitted by the Supply Officer; and c. maintain the supplies ledger cards for each type of supplies and materials for reconciliation with the stock cards of the Supply Officer. Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to: a. prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) together with the original copy of the Requisition and Issue Slips to the Municipal Accountant on a weekly basis; b. maintain stock cards; and c. conduct physical count of supplies at least every semester and prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 for the first and second semesters, respectively, to report the physical count of supplies by type of inventory.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant adopted the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for the receipts and issuances of inventory. The Municipality only used SSMI with RIS as basis in recording of inventory expense for issued office supplies. The Municipality prepared and submitted SSMI to Municipal Accountant for office supplies inventory. Stock Cards were maintained for office supplies only.
observations.reason: Not all inventory accounts were covered with Stock Cards. The MGSO was not able to conduct physical count and prepare and submit the RPCI due to the volume of his existing work.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to prepare a complete RPCPPE
and to submit a copy thereof to the Audit Team not later than January 31 of
eachyear; and
b. Municipal GSO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records as basis
for thelatter toprepare the necessary adjusting entries, if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile the RPT/SET Receivables accounts with the Municipal Treasurer’s List
of RPT/SET Delinquencies and make necessary adjustments, to present fairly the
account’s balanceinthe financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the Municipal Accountant to assess the collectability of the long
outstanding receivables and set-up the corresponding Allowance for
Impairment inthe books of accounts; and
b. if collectability is futile, request to write-off the receivables in accordance
withthe provisions of COACircular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer,
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to exert all efforts to come up with
all the possible remedies to enforce settlement of the loan for a fair presentation
of the account inthefinancial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to
provide the Municipal Accountant with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects to facilitate the proper
disclosure of theroadnetwork system inthe Notes toFinancial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to:
 adopt the Perpetual Inventory Method in accounting for the receipts and
issuances of supplies, in compliance with Sections 114 and 121 of
MNGAS forLGUs, Volume I;
 record the issuances thereof based on the Summary of Supplies and
Materials Issued (SSMI) submitted bytheSupply Officer; and
 maintain the supplies ledger cards for each supplies and materials for
reconciliation with thestock cardsof theSupply Officer.
b. Municipal General Services Officer(MGSO)to:
 prepare and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI)
together with the original copy of the Requisition and Issue Slips to the
Municipal Accountant ona weeklybasis;
 maintain stock cards; and
 conduct physical count of supplies at least every semester and prepare
and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) to the
Office of the Auditor not later than July 31 and January 31 for the first
and second semesters, respectively, to report the physical count of
supplies by type of inventory.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to address the variance in the
reciprocal accounts Due to/from LGUs and make the necessary adjustments to
ensure thatthe balances of theaccounts are inagreement.

We recommended that Management require:
The Inventory Committee to fast-track or expedite the completion of the physical inventory, and thereafter prepare a complete RPCPPE; and
The Accounting Office and the GSO to prioritize and make concerted efforts to reconcile the records maintained for property and equipment in order to ensure the validity, correctness and existence of the Municipality’s PPE as reflected in the financial statements. Likewise, noted discrepancies should be adjusted accordingly.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to record all the transactions of the Municipality in accordance with the prescribed guidelines based on IPSAS 16. Also, we recommended that the following correcting entry be effected:

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to continue and strengthen coordination with the Municipal Engineer to determine the condition and composition of the road network assets constructed in CYs 2015 and below, in order to assess the carrying amounts of the said assets and effect the necessary adjustments in the books to arrive at a more reliable Road Network accounts balance in the financial statements;
Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Inventory Committee with complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to facilitate the preparation of RPCLRN; and
Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the RPCLRN and RLRN, respectively, in accordance with the prescribed formats as provided under COA Circular No. 2015-008.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the GSO prepare and maintain separate subsidiary records/LRNLCs and LRNPCs, respectively, for local road projects to ensure proper recording and control of the assets.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts in retrieving the documents relative to the distribution of construction materials issued to the indigent families and thereafter effect the necessary adjustments in the books; and
Require the GSO and the Municipal Accountant to have proper coordination regarding the issue on the inventory accounts so that the procurement and issuance of the same would be properly recorded and presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Accountant record the purchase and issuance of supplies and materials in accordance with IPSAS No. 12;
GSO ensure that the issued supplies and materials are covered by RIS and SSMI and furnish the Municipal Accountant a copy of the said documents in order for the latter to record the issuance of inventories; and
Local Chief Executive consider the provision of warehouse/storage facility for supplies and materials of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GSO to:
Ensure that all vehicles to be used by the Municipality are in good running condition and the odometer and oil gauges are functioning well;
Instruct the Municipality’s driver to prepare a properly filled out trip ticket and monthly reports of official travels and fuel consumption, and submit the same to the GSO; and
Consolidate the said reports and thereafter submit the consolidated monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the COA Auditor within the ensuing month.

We recommended and Management agreed that:
At the beginning of the year, submit to this office the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year;
Inform this office within 10 days after the award of the infrastructure project or before it commence that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted; and
Ensure the monthly preparation and submission of the monitoring report on PPAs to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that Management ensure that the identified priority PPAs in the Annual LDRRMFIP be implemented to better prepare for and lessen the impact of natural and man-made hazards/disasters and promote socio economic growth in the Municipality.

We recommended that Management comply with the pertinent provisions set forth under DepEd-DSWD-DILG-DOH JMC No. 1 series of 2013 relative to the formulation of an ECM Plan to enable full coordination and ensure timely delivery of adequate and appropriate services to the evacuees in the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Submit its GPB to the DILG Provincial Office for review and endorsement; and
Require the GAD Focal Person to review the pertinent provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC Nos. 2013-01 and 2016-01 to ensure compliance thereto.

We recommended and Management agreed that the provisions of the HGDG and PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 be complied with by subjecting the GAD PPAs to the GAD Checklist during the project identification and designing programs to determine whether the same fully address gender related issues.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the:
The MPDO to spearhead the setting-up and maintenance of GAD database; and
The GAD Focal Point System to ensure full compliance with the above cited rules and regulations by regularly monitoring and expediting the establishment of Local GAD Code.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Fast-track the implementation of all the unimplemented PPAs and ensure that the subsequent projects to be included in the AIP are implemented as scheduled; and
Instruct the Municipal Engineer, the MPDC and the responsible focal person/offices to have coordinated conceptualization and stewardship for future development projects of the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Office of the Municipal Treasurer regularly update the list of delinquent real property taxpayers of the Municipality and enforce a stricter collection policy thereof. Likewise, we recommended that Management continue to implement good collection practices in order to effectively attain the set collection target.

We recommended that Management withhold from the fixed monthly compensation of the concerned personnel the respective outstanding loan balance and remit immediately to the GSIS and ensure a timely withholding and remittance of future monthly dues to avoid penalties, surcharges and interest arising from delayed remittances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant revisit the prior years’ accounting entries made and supporting documents pertaining to the related transactions, and determine the possible erroneous entries made in order to ascertain the cause of the abnormal balances of the Due to BIR and Due to GSIS accounts, and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Management exert extra effort in obtaining all the necessary documents needed to support the identified reconciling items and draw Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment/correction in the books and ensure not to increase the noted reconciling items in the BRS in the ensuing years.

We recommended that Management cause the cancellation of stale checks and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the Cash in Bank and the corresponding Payable and Equity accounts balance in the agency books.

We recommended Management to exercise due diligence by reviewing every details of its transactions prior to disbursement in order to prevent the incurrence of double payments particularly on the payment of loan amortizations since it involves a large amount of money.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of charging expenditures to the LDRRMF which do not directly support the disaster risk reduction management activities of the Municipality. Also, strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth under NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 and other applicable laws, rules and regulations governing the utilization of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that Management ensure that the identified priority PPAs are implemented as planned and to utilize fully the LDRRMF within the scheduled timeframes. In such manner, the in-house capability of the LGU to efficiently manage all types of emergencies and to achieve orderly transitions from response to sustained recovery in case of calamity will be strengthened.
We recommended that Management expedite the revision or updates of the Municipality’s Local Revenue Code through the Local Sanggunian in compliance with the aforementioned law.

We recommended that Management:
Come up with implementable collection strategies to attain much higher collection efficiency on Business Tax, Other Income and Receipts and especially the Real Property Taxes;
Require the MTO to have an updated list of delinquent real property tax payers; and
Conduct a study and implement how to further improve the revenue sources of the Municipality.

to the Bureau of Internal Revenue in accordance with BIR Revenue Regulation No. 2-
98. Likewise, the Municipal Accounting and Treasurer Office should reconcile their
records regularly to avoid non-payment of BIR dues.

The Municipal Accountant asserted that the LGU fully comply the remittance of
taxes withheld. However, the remittance made in the General Fund for the account of Trust
Fund was not yet recognized in the latter book due to the delayed transfer of funds.
Likewise, Management assured that the Accounting Office and the Treasurer Office will
make a reconciliation of their respective records regularly to avoid any discrepancy.

We recommended that Management secure the appropriate environmental
compliance certificate before implementing the declared environmental projects to
avoid project disruption and penalties in compliance with pertinent provisions of PD
No. 1586, DENR Administrative Order No. 2003-30 and DENR EMB MC No. 2015-
005.

Also, we recommended that the BAC during the pre-procurement conference
ensure that all of the required documents for specific projects were already secured
or complied with before initiating the procurement process or activity.

Management ensures that in the future they will follow the strict compliance to
specific laws, rules and regulations in implementing projects. Further, they averred that
before the project implementation they seek advice from the DENR and the latter issued
the CNC and thus the LGU has started to implement the project.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer and the
Municipal Accountant exert extra effort in preparing and submitting on time the
subject accounts and financial reports including the supporting documents. We
suggest that such activities be properly monitored so that reports are submitted
promptly in accordance with the pertinent provisions of PD No. 1445 and COA
Circular No. 2009-006.

Management admitted the late submission of report due to health concern of the
previous Municipal Accountant-ICO. Management ensure to observe prompt submission
of documents and financial reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Officer to prepare LDRRMFIP taking into
consideration the legislated usage of the fund in order that the necessary plans for
risk reduction and proper utilization of the fund can be achieved.

Management was amenable to the audit observation and agreed to comply with our
recommendation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Local Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Council to judiciously plan the
programs/projects/activities to be funded out of the LDRRMF. If warranted,
attendance in trainings and seminars pertaining to calamity activities be likewise
included in the said plan.

Management was amenable to the audit observation and agreed to comply with our
recommendation.

‘We recommended that Management plan thoroughly the SEF budget to avoid
non-implementation of projects/activities. Moreover, the budget should be monitored
strictly to anticipate the timely re-allocation of funds for other allowable activities in
lieu of those that cannot be implemented.

Management was amenable to the audit observation and agreed to comply with our
recommendation.

We recommended that the Local School Board efficiently and effectively
utilize the fund and include the savings from previous years as beginning balance in
the preparation of annual budget and/or a supplemental budget be enacted to finance
the priority projects as embodied in Section 272 of RA 7160.

Management was amenable to the audit observation and agreed to comply with our
recommendation.

We recommended that Management:

a. comply the requirements provided by NSWM in the final approval of Solid
Waste Management Plan and as a guide for the efficient management of
solid waste generated within the Municipality's jurisdiction;

b. implement the safe closure and rehabilitation process of the old dumpsite
at Barangay San Antonio; and

c. prioritize solid waste management programs and projects by
providing/allocating enough budget for their full implementation

Management was amenable to the audit observation and agreed to comply with our
recommendation.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to:

a. attend trainings and seminars pertaining to GAD rules and regulations;

b. strictly observed in the prescribed format and timeline in the preparation
of GAD Plan and Budget as provided in PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA Joint
Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 to ensure timely compliance
thereof.; and

¢. prepare and maintain records on the detailed breakdown of expenditures
pertaining to GAD activities.

Management was amenable to the audit observation and agreed to comply with our

We reiterate our previous year’s audit recommendations which dealt
primarily on the establishment of the GAD Database and the formulation and
approval of the Local GAD Code.

Management was amenable to the audit observation and agreed to comply with our
recommendation.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Development
Council to update the CLUP in accordance with the approved Provincial
Comprehensive Land Use Plan as mandated by Executive Order (EO) No. 72.

The municipality is now on the preliminary process of updating the CLUP.

We recommended that Management ensure the full implementation of the 20
per cent development fund as embodied in the Annual Investment Plan of the
Municipality as required by Paragraph 5.0 of DILG and DBM Joint Circular No.

2011-1 so that the targeted beneficiaries can benefit from these PPAs the soonest
possible time.

Management was amenable to the audit observation and agreed to implement our
recommendation.
